description: Beginner-friendly view of the Agent Trace Replay platform. A trace is a recording of what an AI agent did (tool calls and HTTP requests). A replay re-runs that recording under controlled conditions, optionally injecting fake failures.

options.content:
## Agent Trace Replay — how to read this board

This platform helps you **re-run what an AI agent did**, so you can debug flaky tool calls without guessing from logs alone.

### Plain-language glossary
| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **Trace** | A recording of one agent run: each tool call / HTTP request in order |
| **Manifest** | A cleaned checklist of steps the platform will replay |
| **Replay** | Re-running that checklist with mock dependencies |
| **Step** | One item in the checklist (for example `customer_lookup`) |
| **Failure injection** | Intentionally faking a bad outcome (timeout, HTTP 500, malformed JSON, slow response) during replay |

### How work flows (left → right)
1. **Traces arrive** — agent recordings are uploaded to the API  
2. **Steps are prepared** — the normalizer turns each trace into a replay checklist  
3. **Replays run** — a worker walks each step and records pass/fail  
4. **You inspect outcomes** — charts below show volume, pass/fail, and injected failure types  

**Tip:** After `./scripts/demo.ps1` (or `bash scripts/demo.sh`), wait ~30 seconds, then refresh. Numbers climb as ingest + replay traffic lands in Prometheus.

panels.title: Traces received
panels.description: How many agent recordings were successfully accepted by the ingest API. Higher means more demo or production traffic was loaded.

panels.title: Checklists built
panels.description: Number of replay manifests compiled. Each successful normalize turns one trace into an ordered checklist of steps.

panels.title: Replays finished
panels.description: Total completed or failed replay jobs. A finished replay means every checklist step was attempted.

panels.title: Steps that passed
panels.description: Individual checklist items that behaved as expected during mock replay (no forced failure, or recorded happy path).

panels.title: Steps that failed
panels.description: Checklist items that failed — often because failure injection forced a timeout, HTTP 500, bad JSON, or slow response.

panels.title: 1) Are agent recordings arriving?
panels.description: Shows how quickly traces are accepted by the API. Spikes usually mean someone ran the demo script or uploaded a batch of agent runs. 'success' is good; 'error' means ingest failed validation or infrastructure issues.

panels.title: 2) Are checklists being prepared?
panels.description: After a trace is saved, a background service builds a replay checklist (manifest). 'success' means a checklist was written. 'retry' means the events were not ready yet and the service will try again.

panels.title: 3) Are replays starting and finishing?
panels.description: Create lines show API requests to start a replay. Finished lines show worker outcomes: completed = all steps passed; failed = at least one step failed (often from injected faults).

panels.title: 4) Did each tool step pass or fail?
panels.description: Each checklist item is one step. Passed means the mock call behaved as expected. Failed means the step hit an error — frequently because we injected a fake failure on purpose.

panels.title: 5) What kinds of fake failures did we inject?
panels.description: Failure injection lets you reproduce intermittent production bugs safely. timeout = call never returns in time; http_500 = server error; malformed_json = unreadable body; slow = unexpectedly slow response.

panels.title: 6) How long do mock tool calls take?
panels.description: Latency of individual mock steps during replay. p50 is a typical step; p95 is a slower outlier. Useful for noticing when 'slow' injection or retry storms stretch step times.

panels.title: 7) How long does a full replay take end-to-end?
panels.description: Wall-clock time from claiming a replay job until the last step finishes. Longer bars mean larger checklists or injected delays.

options.content:
### Try this
1. Run `./scripts/demo.ps1` (Windows) or `bash scripts/demo.sh` (Linux/macOS) with Compose up  
2. Come back here and watch the **top counters** move  
3. Open a replay detail in the API: `GET /v1/replays/?limit=5` then `GET /v1/replays/{replay_id}`  
4. Hover the **i** icon on any panel for a deeper description  

Numbers here are **totals since the services started**. Restarting Docker resets counters.

title: Agent Trace Replay — How to read the platform
